---
title: "Regression Housing Price Prediction"
author: "James Compagno"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme: flatly
    orientation: columns
    embed-resources: true
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import plotnine as p9
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score, cross_val_predict, GridSearchCV
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder, PolynomialFeatures
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer, make_column_selector
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, r2_score, make_scorer
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

ames_train = pd.read_csv("/Users/jamescompagno/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Personal/MSBA/GSB_544/Final_Project/gsb-544-fall-2025-regression/train_new.csv")
ames_test = pd.read_csv("/Users/jamescompagno/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Personal/MSBA/GSB_544/Final_Project/gsb-544-fall-2025-regression/test_new.csv")

X = ames_train.drop(["SalePrice", "PID"], axis=1)
y = np.log1p(ames_train["SalePrice"]) 

X_test = ames_test.drop("PID", axis=1)
test_PID = ames_test["PID"]
```

```{python}
#| label: train-model
#| include: false

# log RMSE
def log_rmse(y_true, y_pred):
    y_pred = np.clip(y_pred, 1, None)
    return np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred))

log_rmse_scorer = make_scorer(log_rmse, greater_is_better=False)

# Column Transformer with Polynomial Features
ct_poly = ColumnTransformer(
    [
        ("num", 
         Pipeline([
             ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
             ("scale", StandardScaler()),
             ("poly", PolynomialFeatures(degree=2, interaction_only=True, include_bias=False))
         ]), 
         make_column_selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
        ("cat", 
         Pipeline([
             ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
             ("encode", OneHotEncoder(drop="first", sparse_output=False, handle_unknown="ignore"))
         ]), 
         make_column_selector(dtype_include=object))
    ],
    verbose_feature_names_out=False,
).set_output(transform="pandas")

# Pipeline with Ridge
pipe = Pipeline([
    ("preprocess", ct_poly),
    ("ridge", Ridge())
])

# GridSearchCV
param_grid = {'ridge__alpha': np.logspace(-2, 3, 50)}
grid_search = GridSearchCV(pipe, param_grid, cv=5, scoring=log_rmse_scorer)
grid_search.fit(X, y)

best_model = grid_search.best_estimator_
best_alpha = grid_search.best_params_['ridge__alpha']

# Cross-validation predictions
y_pred_cv = cross_val_predict(best_model, X, y, cv=5)

# Metrics
cv_log_rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y, y_pred_cv))
cv_r2 = r2_score(y, y_pred_cv)
cv_mse = mean_squared_error(y, y_pred_cv)

# Get coefficients
feature_names = best_model.named_steps['preprocess'].get_feature_names_out()
coefficients = best_model.named_steps['ridge'].coef_

coef_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Variable': feature_names,
    'Coefficient': coefficients
}).sort_values('Coefficient', key=abs, ascending=False).head(10)
```

# Overview

## Row {height=20%}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Log RMSE"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_log_rmse:.4f}",
    color="success"
)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "R² Mean"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_r2:.1%}",
    color="primary"
)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "MSE Mean"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_mse:.4f}",
    color="info"
)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Best Alpha"
dict(
    value=f"{best_alpha:.4f}",
    color="secondary"
)
```

## Row {height=40%}

### Column {width=50%}

```{python}
#| title: "Sale Price Distribution"
#| output: true

price_df = pd.DataFrame({'SalePrice': ames_train['SalePrice']})

(p9.ggplot(price_df, p9.aes(x='SalePrice'))
    + p9.geom_histogram(bins=40, fill='#3498db', color='white', alpha=0.8)
    + p9.labs(x='Sale Price ($)', y='Count')
    + p9.scale_x_continuous(labels=lambda x: ['${:,.0f}'.format(v) for v in x])
    + p9.theme(figure_size=(5, 4))
    + p9.theme(
        panel_background=p9.element_rect(fill="white"), 
        panel_grid_major=p9.element_line(color="#f2f7f8", size=0.5)
    )
).draw()
```

### Column {width=50%}

```{python}
#| title: "Predicted vs Actual"
#| output: true

pred_actual_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Actual': np.expm1(y),
    'Predicted': np.expm1(y_pred_cv)
})

(p9.ggplot(pred_actual_df, p9.aes(x='Actual', y='Predicted'))
    + p9.geom_point(alpha=0.4, color='#3498db')
    + p9.geom_abline(intercept=0, slope=1, color='#e74c3c', linetype='dashed', size=1)
    + p9.labs(x='Actual Price ($)', y='Predicted Price ($)')
    + p9.scale_x_continuous(labels=lambda x: ['${:,.0f}'.format(v) for v in x])
    + p9.scale_y_continuous(labels=lambda x: ['${:,.0f}'.format(v) for v in x])
    + p9.theme(figure_size=(5, 4))
    + p9.theme(
        panel_background=p9.element_rect(fill="white"), 
        panel_grid_major=p9.element_line(color="#f2f7f8", size=0.5)
    )
).draw()
```

## Row {height=40%}

### Column {width=50%}

**Model Summary**

- Ridge Regression with polynomial interaction features
- Utilized 5-fold cross-validation
- Log-transformed target variable and RMSE for better predictions

**Best Parameters Found**

- Alpha optimized with gridsearchCV
- Degree 2 polynomial with interaction terms only

**Business Takeaways**

- With an R2 of 88.3% most of the variation in the data is explained
- Living area (Gr Liv Area) was the most important feature
- 3 of the top 10 features were neighborhoods (example: Neighborhood_MeadowV)

### Column {width=50%}

```{python}
#| title: "Top 10 Most Important Features"

coef_df_display = coef_df.copy()
coef_df_display['Coefficient'] = coef_df_display['Coefficient'].round(4)
coef_df_display
```
